title: Image Format Converter
shortDescription: Convert PNG, JPG and WebP images directly in your browser.
metaDescription: Convert PNG, JPG and WebP images in your browser with instant local processing and no server upload.
intro: Convert PNG, JPG, and WebP images directly in your browser and download the new file right away.
overview:
This tool changes an image from one common format to another without sending the file to a remote server. It is useful when you need a different file type for publishing, sharing, archiving, or opening the image in software that accepts only specific formats.

You can move between PNG, JPG/JPEG, and WebP while keeping the process local to your device. That means the original image stays in your browser session, and the converted file is ready as soon as processing finishes.

Use it when you need a practical file you can open, upload, or place into a design workflow without extra steps.
howItWorks: Add an image file from your device or paste one into the tool.
Choose the target format you want to create.
Review the converted preview and the resulting filename.

useCases.title: Turn a product photo into JPG for store listings

useCases.description: Keep transparency intact when converting a logo or UI icon from another format into PNG for design use.

useCases.title: Create a WebP copy for a website asset
useCases.description: Produce a WebP version of a banner or hero image for teams that publish assets in Web-ready formats.

useCases.description: Convert WebP to PNG or JPG when a legacy editor cannot read the original file type.

faq.question: Will transparency stay in the converted file?
faq.answer: Only formats that support transparency can preserve it. PNG keeps transparency, while JPG removes it because the format does not support alpha channels.

faq.question: Why does a JPG look different from the original image?
faq.answer: JPG is a lossy format, so fine details, edges, and flat color areas can change slightly during conversion. That is expected behavior.

faq.question: Can every image be converted to every supported format?
faq.answer: Most standard PNG, JPG/JPEG, and WebP files can be converted, but the output still depends on whether the source image is readable in the browser.

faq.question: What if the converted file name is not what I want?
faq.answer: The tool generates a filename based on the source file and the selected format. You can rename the downloaded file after saving it.
